bibliographic_data.title: They Might NOT Be Giants: Crafting Black-Box Adversarial Examples with Fewer Queries Using Particle Swarm Optimization
bibliographic_data.abstract: Machine learning models have been found to be susceptible to adversarial examples that are often indistinguishable from the original inputs. These adversarial examples are created by applying adversarial perturbations to input samples, which would cause them to be misclassified by the target models. Attacks that search and apply the perturbations to create adversarial examples are performed in both white-box and black-box settings, depending on the information available to the attacker about the target. For black-box attacks, the only capability available to the attacker is the ability to query the target with specially crafted inputs and observing the labels returned by the model. Current black-box attacks either have low success rates, require a high number of queries, or produce adversarial examples that are easily distinguishable from their sources. In this paper, we present AdversarialPSO, a black-box attack that uses fewer queries to create adversarial examples with high success rates. AdversarialPSO is based on the evolutionary search algorithm Particle Swarm Optimization, a population-based gradient-free optimization algorithm. It is flexible in balancing the number of queries submitted to the target vs the quality of imperceptible adversarial examples. The attack has been evaluated using the image classification benchmark datasets CIFAR-10, MNIST, and Imagenet, achieving success rates of 99.6%, 96.3%, and 82.0%, respectively, while submitting substantially fewer queries than the state-of-the-art.

core_content.novelty_claim: AdversarialPSO uses Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to create black-box adversarial examples with fewer queries and high success rates.
core_content.key_methodology: AdversarialPSO is based on PSO, a population-based gradient-free optimization algorithm. Particles in the swarm make small perturbations to input images that are virtually imperceptible to humans. The method balances the number of queries submitted to the target model with the quality of the adversarial examples generated.
outcomes: Achieved success rates of 99.6%, 96.3%, and 82.0% on CIFAR-10, MNIST, and Imagenet respectively while using fewer queries than state-of-the-art methods.
Presented SWISS (Swarms with Individual Search Spaces) as a method to isolate salient features in black-box settings.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Machine learning models are susceptible to adversarial examples that can cause misclassification. This paper introduces AdversarialPSO, which uses Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to create black-box adversarial examples with fewer queries and high success rates. PSO is a population-based gradient-free optimization algorithm where particles make small perturbations to input images. The method balances the number of queries submitted to the target model with the quality of the adversarial examples generated. AdversarialPSO was evaluated on CIFAR-10, MNIST, and Imagenet, achieving success rates of 99.6%, 96.3%, and 82.0% respectively while using fewer queries than state-of-the-art methods. The paper also presents SWISS (Swarms with Individual Search Spaces) as a method to isolate salient features in black-box settings.
